title: Reproduce the WSSV circular conservation ring comparison in the web app
summary: Build a single-reference circular White spot syndrome virus diagram with 20 conservation rings generated from browser LOSAT blastn results.
primaryWorkflow: Circular conservation rings with browser LOSAT blastn
requirements: Enough available memory for one WSSV GenBank record, 20 comparison FASTA files, and LOSAT blastn output.
The reference GenBank file and comparison FASTA files listed below.
Use a local copy of gbdraw.app or the hosted web app; genome processing runs in the browser.

downloads.label: AP027280
downloads.note: Download the JP02 White spot syndrome virus record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: CN01
downloads.note: Prepare the complete comparison genome sequence as FASTA.

quickReproduce.title: Download the Gallery session
quickReproduce.body: Use the Session button in this Gallery entry to save WSSV_genome_comparison.gbdraw-session.json.

media.alt: Gallery header for the WSSV circular conservation ring comparison with the Session action visible.
media.caption: The Gallery entry keeps the finished interactive SVG, downloadable SVG, and session JSON together.

operations.title: Session button for this Gallery entry

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing session button for this gallery entry.
media.caption: Session button for this Gallery entry in the gallery page view.

quickReproduce.title: Open the web app
quickReproduce.body: Open gbdraw.app, then load the saved .gbdraw-session.json from the session controls.

operations.title: Header session controls
operations.body: Use the rendered web app header toolbar and click Load Session.

media.alt: Actual Circular web app header toolbar with Load Session, Save Session, Reset Settings, undo and redo, the session title, and mode controls visible.
media.caption: The rendered .app-toolbar crop shows the Load Session control in its real header-toolbar context.

quickReproduce.title: Inspect the restored conservation stack
quickReproduce.body: The session restores the AP027280 GenBank input, comparison FASTA files, cached LOSAT results, conservation series labels and colors, the circular layout, and the generated interactive SVG.

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing restored conservation stack.
media.caption: Restored conservation stack in the generated preview view.

manualSteps.title: Switch to Circular mode
manualSteps.body: Open gbdraw.app and select Circular mode before adding the WSSV reference record.

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing mode segmented control.
media.caption: Mode segmented control in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Upload the reference GenBank file
manualSteps.body: Upload AP027280.gb as the circular reference. This record is the JP02 White spot syndrome virus genome shown at the center of the Gallery figure.
items: Features: CDS, rRNA, tRNA, tmRNA, ncRNA, repeat_region

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing circular genbank uploader.
media.caption: Circular GenBank uploader in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Set the basic circular layout
manualSteps.body: Use a spread-out circular track so the outer CDS features remain readable around the conservation rings.

operations.title: Basic circular layout controls
operations.body: Confirm spreadout, legend left, suppress GC/skew.

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing basic circular layout controls.
media.caption: Basic circular layout controls in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Open Circular Conservation
manualSteps.body: Enable circular conservation, then upload the 20 comparison FASTA files in the order used by the Gallery session.
items: Source: Run LOSAT from FASTA inputs
manualSteps.note: If you import precomputed conservation BLAST tables instead of running browser LOSAT, use the subject/reference orientation expected by those tables.

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing circular conservation panel.
media.caption: Circular Conservation panel in the web app session view.

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing conservation fasta uploader.
media.caption: Conservation FASTA uploader in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Name the conservation rings
manualSteps.body: Assign one short label per FASTA so the left legend and each ring order match the Gallery figure.
items: CN01, WSSV-TW, WSSV-CN, WSSV-TH, JP01A, JP01B, Pc2020, E1, 0722-1, CN03
CN04, WSSV-AU, EU129, GCF7, MES-753, Shantou2019, POMZ1, POMZ4, MG18PR-0187-N40S, Angostura2013

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing ring label controls.
media.caption: Ring label controls in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Set ring colors
manualSteps.body: Use distinct ring colors so conservation gaps can be compared by eye across the full 20-genome stack.

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing ring color controls.
media.caption: Ring color controls in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Set conservation thresholds
manualSteps.body: Use strict blastn thresholds so the rings show high-confidence conserved nucleotide segments.

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing conservation thresholds.
media.caption: Conservation thresholds in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Run browser LOSAT
manualSteps.body: Use Auto execution with a Safe total thread budget for the first run. The saved session includes cached LOSAT output; manual rebuilds need to run the FASTA comparisons once.

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing browser losat execution controls.
media.caption: Browser LOSAT execution controls in the web app session view.

manualSteps.title: Generate the diagram
manualSteps.body: Click Generate Diagram. The preview should show the AP027280 feature ring, 20 conservation rings, the position scale, and a left legend with an identity gradient for each comparison genome.

media.alt: Generated WSSV circular diagram with 20 colored conservation rings and a left identity legend.
media.caption: The finished figure stacks 20 blastn-backed conservation rings inside the reference CDS feature ring.

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing generated conservation rings.
media.caption: Generated conservation rings in the generated preview view.

manualSteps.title: Check the Files tab
manualSteps.body: Use the Gallery Files tab to confirm the required input names, session JSON, interactive SVG, and thumbnail for this example.

media.alt: Actual Circular Gallery tab bar with Files selected above the Files panel.
media.caption: The rendered Gallery .tab-bar stays visible with Files selected above the file and artifact list.

operations.title: Files tab inputs
operations.body: Open the Gallery Files tab; the selected tab remains visible above the file list.

media.alt: Actual Circular Gallery tab bar with Files selected above the Files panel.
media.caption: The rendered Gallery .tab-bar stays visible with Files selected above the file and artifact list.

manualSteps.title: Inspect a CDS feature
manualSteps.body: Click an outer CDS feature in the interactive SVG to inspect the label, location, product, protein_id, and source qualifiers.

media.alt: WSSV conservation ring diagram with a CDS feature popup open.
media.caption: Feature clicks are useful for checking which annotation field is driving labels and colors.

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing cds feature popup.
media.caption: CDS feature popup in the popup view.

colorRules.title: Feature colors and ring colors are separate
colorRules.body: The CDS ring uses the royal_gala feature palette. Conservation ring colors are configured per comparison FASTA and are not feature color rules.

colorRules.title: Use the identity gradient legend
colorRules.body: Each conservation ring uses a light-to-strong gradient for nucleotide identity. In this Gallery figure the useful displayed range is 90% to 100%.

colorRules.title: Keep ring labels short
colorRules.body: Short labels such as CN01 or WSSV-TW keep the 20-row legend readable. Put longer isolate details in your notes or figure caption.

colorRules.title: Use feature rules only for reference annotations
colorRules.body: If you add feature-specific colors, make them from AP027280 qualifiers such as note, product, locus_tag, or protein_id. They will not recolor the conservation rings.

postGenerationEdits.title: Use zoom before editing
postGenerationEdits.body: This SVG is large. Zoom or pan the preview before clicking small CDS features or inspecting narrow conservation gaps.

operations.title: Zoom controls
operations.body: Use the floating preview toolbar for zoom, reset, layout editing, or canvas padding.

media.alt: Actual WSSV web app floating preview controls with Reset Layout, Layout edit, Canvas padding, and zoom buttons visible.
media.caption: The rendered floating preview toolbar shows zoom together with reset, layout edit, and canvas padding controls.

postGenerationEdits.title: Click features to verify annotations
postGenerationEdits.body: Use the feature popup to confirm note, product, protein_id, location, and sequence metadata before changing feature color or label settings.

operations.title: CDS feature popup
operations.body: Confirm annotation fields before changing feature colors or labels.

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing CDS feature popup metadata.
media.caption: Feature popup metadata identifies the annotation fields behind labels and colors.

postGenerationEdits.title: Keep the conservation legend visible
postGenerationEdits.body: The left legend explains ring order and the identity gradient. If you move or edit the legend, confirm that the 90% and 100% endpoints remain visible.

media.alt: WSSV conservation tutorial operation screenshot showing conservation legend.
media.caption: Conservation legend in the generated preview view.

postGenerationEdits.title: Export SVG first
postGenerationEdits.body: Download SVG when you need editable vector output. PNG and PDF export can take longer for this large, detailed interactive figure.

operations.title: Export controls
operations.body: Use the rendered Preview export toolbar for SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, or PDF.

media.alt: Actual Circular Preview export toolbar with the DPI selector and SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, and PDF export buttons visible.
media.caption: The rendered .preview-toolbar crop shows DPI plus SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, and PDF export controls.

postGenerationEdits.title: Save a session
postGenerationEdits.body: Save a .gbdraw-session.json after edits so the web app can restore inputs, LOSAT cache, conservation series settings, generated output, and SVG adjustments.

operations.title: Session save controls
operations.body: Use the rendered web app header toolbar and click Save Session.

media.alt: Actual Circular web app header toolbar with Save Session, Load Session, Reset Settings, undo and redo, the session title, and mode controls visible.
media.caption: The rendered .app-toolbar crop shows Save Session in the same header toolbar used to load sessions.

losatTips.title: Use blastn megablast for close viral genomes
losatTips.body: The WSSV comparison uses nucleotide-level conservation, so blastn with megablast is the appropriate browser LOSAT workflow.

losatTips.title: Start with Auto execution
losatTips.body: Auto uses threaded LOSAT only when the browser supports it and the workload justifies it.

losatTips.title: Prefer Safe total threads
losatTips.body: Safe avoids consuming every logical CPU and is the best default for keeping the browser responsive during 20 FASTA comparisons.

losatTips.title: Use Serial on constrained browsers
losatTips.body: If threaded WebAssembly is unavailable or the tab becomes unresponsive, switch Execution to Serial before rerunning.

losatTips.title: Reuse the cache
losatTips.body: Raw LOSAT results are reused when the canonical inputs and LOSAT settings match, so styling-only changes should be faster after the first run.

troubleshooting.title: Conservation rings are missing
troubleshooting.body: Confirm Circular Conservation is enabled, comparison FASTA files are uploaded, source is set to Run LOSAT from FASTA inputs, and the generated result is the circular output.

troubleshooting.title: A ring appears in the wrong order
troubleshooting.body: Confirm the FASTA upload order, ring labels, and ring colors. The legend follows the configured conservation series order.

troubleshooting.title: The rings look too sparse
troubleshooting.body: The Gallery figure uses strict thresholds. Lower identity or alignment length only when you intentionally want weaker matches to appear.

troubleshooting.title: The browser becomes slow
troubleshooting.body: Use the Gallery session for inspection, close other heavy tabs, run LOSAT with Safe or Serial settings, or temporarily use fewer comparison FASTA files while tuning layout.

troubleshooting.title: Threaded LOSAT is unavailable
troubleshooting.body: Use Auto or Serial execution. Some browser contexts disable SharedArrayBuffer or cross-origin isolation, which prevents threaded WebAssembly.

troubleshooting.title: The generated diagram differs from the Gallery image
troubleshooting.body: Confirm AP027280.gb, spreadout track type, separate strands, suppressed GC and skew tracks, conservation ring width and gap, thresholds, labels, colors, and comparison FASTA order.
